User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user within the BookYourBrands CRM system.

role.description: The user's role within the system (admin, team, client).

profilePic.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a project managed within the CRM.

clientId.description: Reference to the Client associated with this project. (Relationship: Client 1:N Project)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the project was created.

status.description: The current status of the project.

Task.title: Task
Task.description: Represents a task within a project.

projectId.description: Reference to the Project this task belongs to. (Relationship: Project 1:N Task)

assignedTo.description: Reference to the User assigned to this task. (Relationship: User 1:N Task)

dueDate.description: The due date for the task.

status.description: The current status of the task (Pending, In Progress, Completed).

Chat.title: Chat
Chat.description: Represents a chat room for a project.

id.description: Unique identifier for the chat (matches projectId).

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents a message within a chat.

chatId.description: Reference to the Chat this message belongs to. (Relationship: Chat 1:N Message)

senderId.description: Reference to the User who sent the message. (Relationship: User 1:N Message)

text.description: The text content of the message.

timestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the message was sent.

fileURL.description: URL to a file attached to the message (if any).

File.title: File
File.description: Represents a file uploaded to the system.

projectId.description: Reference to the Project this file is associated with. (Relationship: Project 1:N File)

uploadedBy.description: Reference to the User who uploaded the file. (Relationship: User 1:N File)

fileURL.description: URL to the file in Firebase Storage.

definition.description: Stores user data including role (admin, team, client).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores project data. Includes denormalized 'clientId' for authorization independence and a 'members' map containing user roles for the project (admin, team, client).

params.name: projectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the project.

definition.description: Stores task data. Includes denormalized 'projectId' and 'assignedTo' for authorization independence.

params.name: taskId
params.description: The unique identifier of the task.

definition.description: Stores chat room data. The 'chatId' matches the 'projectId'.

params.name: chatId
params.description: The unique identifier of the chat (project ID).

definition.description: Stores messages within a chat room. Includes denormalized 'chatId' and 'senderId'.

params.name: chatId
params.description: The unique identifier of the chat (project ID).

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the message.

definition.description: Stores file metadata. Includes denormalized 'projectId' and 'uploadedBy'.

params.name: fileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the file.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure security, scalability, and ease of debugging, following the principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, and DBAC. It leverages denormalization and structural segregation to simplify security rules and enable secure list operations. The design addresses the user's requirement for role-based access (admin, team, client) and management of clients, projects, tasks, chats, and files.

Authorization Independence:
Authorization context is denormalized to avoid `get()` calls in security rules. For example, the `Project` collection includes the `members` map, denormalizing team and client roles. Tasks include the projectId. This ensures that authorization checks do not rely on fetching parent document data, which would break atomic operations.

Structural Segregation:
Data with different access requirements are segregated into distinct collections. User data, project data, task data and chat messages are each stored in different collections to allow for simple security rules.

QAPs (Rules are not Filters):
The structure supports secure `list` operations by ensuring each collection has a homogeneous security posture. The project's `members` map allows secure listing of projects accessible to a user, as membership data is directly available within each project document.

Access Modeling:
Path-Based ownership is used for entities representing a User 1:N relationship (e.g. Projects, Tasks, Messages). Collaborative data relies on the Membership Map (`members: {uid1: "role", uid2: "role"}`).
